reasoning: Both models correctly identified the PKGBUILD as safe, showing no malicious code execution during build/install. The package only downloads a pinned binary from official GitHub releases via HTTPS and performs standard installation. SKIP checksum is an accepted practice for nightly binaries.
coverage_issues: Both models only reviewed PKGBUILD. No evidence they checked for .install scripts or other package hooks that might execute during installation, though none appear present in the report context.
false_negatives: Potential risk of unreviewed prebuilt binary contents (though beyond AUR audit scope). Models didn't flag SKIP checksum as requiring extra user caution beyond 'hygiene concern'.
learnings: Improve file selection logic to always check for .install scripts and hooks/* even if not explicitly referenced
Enhance model prompts to explicitly consider checksum hygiene implications for user decisions
Implement cross-model verification for negative findings (confirm absence of install hooks)
